title: ESA Persistent-State Schema (esa-persistent-state-v1.0)
description: Schema for the European Strategic Autonomy monitor's `persistent-state.json` — the canonical knowledge-base file the Applier writes to. The strict layer is the `_meta` block. Sprint-5 Phase 3 (BRIEF cf14eabd, E10 cure) adds a top-level `arrays` block declaring, for every persistent-state array ESA cares about, the merge `key_field` and a `key_constraint` (regex pattern OR string enum). Critical fix: `eu_legislation_tracker[].id` is pattern-locked to `^LEG-\d{3}$` — this is the structural cure for E10 (LEG→EUL key-scheme rotation that caused DisjointKeySchemeError). The engine reads these declarations to enforce additive merge semantics and raise `DisjointKeySchemeError` on key-scheme rotation.

schema_version.description: Schema version of this file. Canonical value: `esa-persistent-state-v1.0`.

last_updated.description: Most recent material state change. ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) or full ISO-8601 timestamp. Bumped by the Applier on cycles that land at least one patch; NOT bumped on null cycles.

last_issue.description: Issue counter. The Applier increments this by 1 on cycles that land at least one patch when the current value is an integer.

eu_legislation_tracker.describe: EU legislative and regulatory instruments relevant to strategic autonomy. Pattern lock `^LEG-\d{3}$` is the structural cure for E10: a prior interpreter output rotated the key scheme to `EUL-NNN`, triggering DisjointKeySchemeError. This lock enforces the LEG-NNN scheme and prevents future silent key-scheme rotation. New legislation entries must use LEG-NNN identifiers.

state_capture_cases.describe: Per-country state-capture severity assessments. Enum-locked to ISO-3166-1 alpha-2. Covers EU27 member states plus UK (GB), US, Ukraine (UA), Georgia (GE), Moldova (MD), Western Balkans candidate states (RS, ME, MK, AL, BA), and key associated/neighbourhood countries (NO, CH, TR, BY). Prevents the Interpreter from free-texting country names (e.g. 'Hungary' vs 'HU') which would create disjoint entries.

member_state_risk_matrix.describe: Per-member-state risk matrix covering foreign dependency, veto risk, and trajectory. Enum-locked to ISO-3166-1 alpha-2 (same set as state_capture_cases). Declared here for Phase 3 coverage-gate compliance — currently an interpreter output; engine merge semantics apply on promotion to persistent-state top-level.

active_actor_campaigns.describe: Foreign-actor campaign entries nested under each actor key (RU, CN, US, IL, TR, GCC, etc.). Campaign `id` follows the `XX-NNN` scheme (e.g. RU-001, CN-001, IL-003) — pattern lock `^[A-Z]{2,3}-\d{3}$` prevents scheme rotation to free-form names or alternative separators.

module_13.items.describe: Module 13 (political economy / member state spotlight) items array. Each entry carries a `country` field — enum-locked to ISO-3166-1 alpha-2 (same set as state_capture_cases and member_state_risk_matrix) to enforce consistent country-key discipline across ESA structured outputs.

key_judgments.describe: ESA key-judgments array from interpreter output. `id` is currently free-form (no pattern established in live state). Forward-declared here so the Phase 3 coverage gate exits clean. If/when a stable id scheme (e.g. ESA-KJ-NNN) is adopted, this constraint should be tightened in a follow-up schema bump.

active_elections.describe: Active election tracking entries keyed by country ISO-3166-1 alpha-2. Declared for ESA BRIEF §3b completeness — `active_elections` lives in persistent-state but is not a top-level interpreter-schema array; forward-declared to lock country-key scheme and prevent name drift (e.g. 'Hungary' vs 'HU').

calibration_log.describe: Append-only calibration log (one entry per material methodology change). `date` is ISO date or full ISO-8601 timestamp — the engine treats this array as additive-only by construction.

gaps_register.describe: Open-findings register for ESA. `gap_id` follows the GAP-NNN scheme (e.g. GAP-001, GAP-002). Pattern lock prevents free-form gap identifiers.
